sn.s: D'Schwiizer gönd am liebschte i d'Bärg go wandere.
sn.e: The Swiss most love going hiking in the mountains.

sn.s: Am Wucheänd packe ich mis Rucksäckli und gaa los.
sn.e: At the weekend I pack my little rucksack and set off.
With good shoes you get far.

sn.s: D'Wäg sind guet beschilderet, mer verlauft sich chuum.
sn.e: The trails are well signposted, you hardly get lost.

sn.s: Es gelbs Schild zeigt, wie lang's no gaht.
sn.e: A yellow sign shows how long it still takes.
Along the way people greet each other: "Hello everyone!"

sn.s: Au wildfremdi Lüt säged Grüezi, das ghört eifach so.
sn.e: Even total strangers say hello, that's just how it is.

sn.s: Obe am Gipfel mues ich tüüf schnuufe.
sn.e: Up at the summit I have to breathe deeply.

sn.s: D'Ussicht über de See und d'Bärg isch unglaublich.
sn.e: The view over the lake and the mountains is incredible.

sn.s: Ich ässe mis Brot und gnüsse d'Rueh.
sn.e: I eat my bread and enjoy the quiet.

sn.s: Da obe fühlt mer sich chliin und frei zglich.
sn.e: Up there you feel small and free at the same time.

sn.s: D'Nature isch da nöch und ghört zum Läbe.
sn.e: Nature is close here and is part of life.

sn.s: Wandere isch nöd nu Sport. Es isch e Art z'läbe.
sn.e: Hiking isn't just sport. It's a way of living.

sn.s: Jetz verstah ich, warum d'Schwiizer ihri Bärg so liebed.
sn.e: Now I understand why the Swiss love their mountains so much.
